title: What are the safe tank gauging...

messages.content: What are the safe tank gauging practices under OSHA 3843?

Under OSHA 3843, safe tank gauging practices include [OSHA 3843, Page 6]:
* Training workers in proper work practices, such as tank-opening procedures, to minimize risks for exposures
* Ensuring intrinsic safety by proper grounding and prohibiting the use of spark-producing devices or equipment
* Establishing administrative controls to reduce the number of times throughout a shift a worker is required to manually gauge tanks
* Safely reducing tank pressure prior to gauging
* Using appropriate respiratory protection, including a supplied air respirator (SAR) and/or self-contained breathing apparatus (SCBA) in areas where IDLH VOC exposures may occur
* Providing hazard communication training in a language that employees understand to ensure that general site workers, tank gaugers and samplers are aware of the hazards associated with tank gauging [OSHA 3843, Page 6]. 

Additionally, employers should consult with a trained occupational safety and health professional to determine the appropriate respirator to be used and to ensure that an appropriate air-sampling strategy is used [OSHA 3843, Page 6].
